metric_value: 3 principal failures: annual tactic changes, technical execution failures, ISV attrition

metric_value: NT Server unit sales ramping; PC server suppliers reporting NetWare sales decline vs NT growth

metric_value: further erosion of Novell enterprise position projected

metric_value: Microsoft OLE Directory Services will succeed because OLE-enabled applications drag it into enterprise

metric_value: LDAP will become de-facto directory services standard for Internet

metric_value: little evidence NDS will achieve widespread acceptance outside Novell installed base

metric_value: acquired by Attachmate Group for $2.2B in April 2011; 882 patents sold to Microsoft consortium for $450M
notes: Novell independence ended exactly as Aberdeen predicted; NetWare displaced by Windows Server. Attachmate later acquired by Micro Focus (2014), then OpenText (2023)

metric_value: Windows NT and successors (2000/2003) became dominant enterprise network OS; NetWare marginalized
notes: Windows NT won the enterprise NOS war exactly as Aberdeen predicted in August 1995

metric_value: LDAP became universal directory protocol; embedded in Active Directory, OpenLDAP, all major systems
notes: Aberdeen LDAP prediction proved exactly right; LDAP is foundational internet infrastructure today

metric_value: GroupWise lost groupware war to Exchange/Notes; retained only legacy Novell-loyal customers
notes: GroupWise market share collapsed as Exchange dominated; consistent with Aberdeen prediction

metric_name: Green River (NetWare next release) expected features

metric_value: Must make NDS best on NT bar none; open new markets to dominate; embrace NT not fight it
